proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_015 (range 30-90, plausibly age) is monotonically associated with pfs_months across the full cohort.
Three-level feature_001 is associated with pfs_months such that higher levels (0 < 1 < 2) correspond to progressively shorter pfs_months (consistent with a performance-status-like ordering).
Binary feature_014 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_014 = 0.
Binary feature_027 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_027 = 0 (mean difference > 0.5 months).
Continuous feature_025 (range 0-24.6) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; rho, p = stats.spearmanr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.785 between feature_015 and pfs_months (p ~ 0). Decile means rise monotonically from 1.33 mo (lowest decile of feature_015, 30-52) to 7.45 mo (highest decile, 78-90). Linear slope +0.176 mo per unit of feature_015.

ANOVA across three levels of feature_001 yields F = 3528.9, p ~ 0. Means: level 0 = 5.27 mo (n=17592), level 1 = 4.05 mo (n=24971), level 2 = 2.91 mo (n=7437). Direction is monotonically negative as level increases.
Welch t-test: pfs_months mean is 5.05 in feature_014=0 (n=22519) vs 3.70 in feature_014=1 (n=27481), difference = -1.352 (p = 0.0).
pfs_months mean = 4.12 in feature_027=0 (n=39978) vs 5.09 in feature_027=1 (n=10022); difference = +0.972 (Welch t-test p = 1.7e-216).
Spearman rho = -0.120 between feature_025 and pfs_months (p = 5.6e-160).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_019 (median 3.80, range 1.5-5.5; plausibly serum albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months.
Continuous feature_005 (median 4.44, right-skewed range 0.02-777) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
Binary feature_006 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006 = 0.
Binary feature_023 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023 = 0.
Binary feature_022 = 1 (rare, ~5% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_022 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.103 between feature_019 and pfs_months (p = 4.4e-117). In multivariable OLS, every 1-unit increase in feature_019 raises pfs_months by 0.469 (p ~ 0).

Spearman rho = -0.047 between feature_005 and pfs_months (p = 1.3e-25). Adjusted OLS slope -0.005 per unit (p = 1.3e-97), consistent with a small negative main effect — large because of feature_005's heavy right tail.
Welch t-test: feature_006=0 mean 4.42 vs feature_006=1 mean 4.11 (n=17657); difference = -0.305 (p = 1.0e-49).
Welch t-test: feature_023=0 mean 4.45 vs feature_023=1 mean 4.12 (n=20898); difference = -0.327 (p = 1.9e-58).
Welch t-test: feature_022=0 mean 4.32 vs feature_022=1 mean 4.09 (n=2272); difference = -0.228 (p = 3.5e-7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is approximately balanced with respect to all other measured features (no covariate has a chi-square or t-test p < 0.05 vs feature_027), consistent with a randomized treatment indicator rather than a prognostic biomarker.

Even after adjusting for all other features, feature_027 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_027 = 0 with adjusted effect of approximately +0.94 months (i.e., the unadjusted main effect is not confounded).

analyses.result_summary: In bivariate tests of feature_027 vs all 32 other features, no covariate showed an imbalance with p < 0.05 (smallest p ~ 0.09 for feature_024 and feature_018). Continuous variables differ in mean by < 1% across feature_027 strata. Pattern is consistent with a randomized treatment assignment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_014 + feature_006 + feature_023 + ...', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS with all 32 covariates: feature_027 coefficient = +0.943 (p ~ 0). The main-effect estimate is essentially unchanged from the unadjusted +0.972, confirming no confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is modified by feature_005 such that feature_027 produces a larger pfs_months gain in patients with low feature_005 (e.g., <= median) than in patients with high feature_005.
The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is suppressed when feature_023 = 1 relative to feature_023 = 0.
The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is suppressed when feature_006 = 1 relative to feature_006 = 0.
The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is suppressed when feature_022 = 1 relative to feature_022 = 0.
The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is enhanced when feature_011 = 1 relative to feature_011 = 0.

analyses.code: df['lo5']=(df['feature_005']<=df['feature_005'].median()).astype(int); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027*lo5', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p = 3.7e-244. Treatment effect of feature_027: +1.806 months when feature_005 <= median (4.44), +0.135 months when feature_005 > median.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_023)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p = 7.7e-226. Treatment effect: +1.656 in feature_023=0 vs +0.027 in feature_023=1 (i.e., effect essentially abolished when feature_023=1).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_006)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p = 4.1e-168. Treatment effect: +1.480 in feature_006=0 vs +0.026 in feature_006=1 (essentially abolished when feature_006=1).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_022)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p = 6.1e-23. Treatment effect: +1.027 in feature_022=0 vs -0.156 in feature_022=1 (effect goes slightly negative when feature_022=1).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_011)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p = 1.2e-11. Treatment effect: +0.941 in feature_011=0 vs +1.929 in feature_011=1 (i.e., enhanced effect in the rare 3% with feature_011=1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After feature_005, feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, and feature_011, no remaining feature in the dataset shows a statistically significant treatment-by-feature interaction at p < 0.001 with feature_027.

analyses.code: for c in features: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027*X', df).fit() where X is c (median split if continuous)
analyses.result_summary: Comprehensive screen of treatment-by-feature interactions yields five strong modifiers (all p < 1e-10): feature_005, feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, feature_011. The next most significant interactions are feature_019 (p=6.7e-3) and feature_013 (p=4.2e-2), both negligible in magnitude (effect difference < 0.15 months). All other features have p_int > 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is strongly enriched (>= +4 months) only in patients meeting all three of: feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005 <= 4.44 (median); in any other combination of these three the effect is < 0.5 months.

analyses.code: Stratified means of pfs_months by feature_027 within each of the 8 cells defined by (feature_006, feature_023, feature_005<=4.44).
analyses.result_summary: All-favorable cell (feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_005<=4.44; n=9379): treated mean 8.73, untreated 4.09, treatment effect = +4.644 (p ~ 0). The other 7 cells: treatment effect ranges from -0.06 to +0.30 months (mostly p > 0.1). Confirms the joint subgroup hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0), the treatment effect of feature_027 has a sharp threshold around feature_005 ~ 5; below the threshold the effect is approximately +4.6 months and above it the effect is essentially nil.

analyses.code: Sweep cuts of feature_005 within feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 stratum.
analyses.result_summary: Decile-wise treatment effects within the favorable f006/f023 stratum: deciles 1-5 of feature_005 (cuts <= 4.49) show treatment effects of +4.5 to +4.8. Decile 6 (4.49-6.05) shows +1.53. Deciles 7-10 (>6.05) show effects between -0.08 and +0.01. Sharpest single threshold falls between 4.9 (eff +4.4) and 5.0 (eff +4.3). Effect is essentially zero for feature_005 > 5.0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005 <= 5, feature_022 = 1 fully suppresses the treatment effect of feature_027.

analyses.code: Subset to feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 & feature_005<=5 & feature_022 in {0,1}.
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 & feature_005<=5: treatment effect = +5.014 in feature_022=0 (n=9338) vs -0.165 in feature_022=1 (n=779). The treatment effect is essentially abolished by feature_022=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is approximately +5.0 months in the joint favorable subgroup defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005 <= 5.0 AND feature_022=0; in the complement (any predicate violated) the treatment effect is essentially zero.

analyses.result_summary: Joint favorable subgroup (n=9338, 18.7%): treatment effect of feature_027 = +5.014 (treated mean 9.10 vs untreated 4.09, p ~ 0). Complement (n=40662): treatment effect = +0.024 (p=0.36). Essentially all of the marginal +0.94 month treatment effect is driven by this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four predicates (feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_005<=5, feature_022=0) is individually NECESSARY for the treatment effect: switching any one predicate alone (with the other three favorable) drops the treatment effect to ~0.

analyses.code: Hold three predicates favorable; flip the 4th and re-estimate.
analyses.result_summary: Starting from all favorable (eff=+5.014): flipping feature_006 to 1 -> eff=+0.113 (p=0.12); flipping feature_023 to 1 -> eff=+0.068 (p=0.26); flipping feature_005 to >5 -> eff=-0.002 (p=0.97); flipping feature_022 to 1 -> eff=-0.165 (p=0.38). Each of the four predicates is necessary: each one alone is sufficient to abolish the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four effect modifiers (feature_006, feature_023, feature_005, feature_022) are PREDICTIVE of treatment benefit, not prognostic: in the untreated cohort (feature_027=0) none of them is significantly associated with pfs_months after adjusting for other prognostic factors.

analyses.code: OLS on untreated subset (feature_027=0) with all candidate covariates
analyses.result_summary: In the untreated subset (n=39978), adjusted coefficients are: feature_006 = +0.003 (p=0.09), feature_023 = -0.002 (p=0.46), feature_022 = +0.002 (p=0.74), feature_011 = -0.004 (p=0.48), feature_005 = -1e-5 (p=0.82). None reaches significance. In the treated subset they are highly significant (e.g., feature_006 -1.47, feature_023 -1.76, feature_022 -1.76, all p < 1e-50). Thus these features ARE NOT prognostic at baseline; they are pure treatment-effect modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest INTRINSIC prognostic factors for pfs_months in this cohort (i.e., associated with pfs_months independent of treatment) are feature_015 (continuous, positive: +0.176/unit), feature_001 (3-level, monotonically negative), feature_014 (binary, negative: -1.37), and feature_019 (continuous, positive: +0.48/unit).

analyses.code: OLS on untreated subset with all candidate covariates
analyses.result_summary: Untreated-only OLS coefficients (all p ~ 0): feature_015 +0.1757 per unit; feature_001 level 1 = -1.185, level 2 = -2.314 vs reference 0; feature_014 = -1.367; feature_019 = +0.483; feature_025 = -0.077; feature_021 = -0.0005. All four primary prognostic factors confirmed; feature_025 and feature_021 are weaker but still significant prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the treated favorable subgroup (feature_027=1 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005<=5 AND feature_022=0), feature_015 (age-like) is positively and approximately linearly associated with pfs_months: the magnitude of treatment-induced PFS extension grows with feature_015.

analyses.code: qcut feature_015 into quartiles within treated-favorable subset
analyses.result_summary: Within treated-favorable (n=1905), pfs_months by feature_015 quartile: Q1 (30-58.1) = 6.80, Q2 (58.1-65.1) = 8.44, Q3 (65.1-72.1) = 9.71, Q4 (72.1-90) = 11.50. Monotonic and approximately linear.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the treated favorable subgroup, feature_001 retains a monotonic negative effect on pfs_months (level 0 best, level 2 worst), so the treatment does not eliminate the prognostic gradient of feature_001.

analyses.code: Treated-favorable subset; group means by feature_001 level
analyses.result_summary: Treated-favorable means: feature_001 = 0 -> 9.97 mo (n=718); = 1 -> 8.90 mo (n=918); = 2 -> 7.51 mo (n=269). Monotonic; feature_001 retains its ordering even on treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup defined by (feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_005<=5, feature_022=0), no continuous feature shows a clinically meaningful treatment-effect modification (interaction effect difference < 0.3 months at median split).

analyses.code: Within favorable subgroup, regress pfs_months ~ feature_027 * (median(c)>med) for each continuous c.
analyses.result_summary: Within the favorable subgroup, median-split interaction tests for all 16 continuous features yield interaction p > 0.05 except feature_032 (p = 0.019; effect difference low=+4.89 vs high=+5.14, magnitude 0.25 months). No other meaningful modifier identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, feature_011 = 1 modestly enhances the treatment effect of feature_027 from approximately +5.0 to approximately +5.2 months, but the magnitude of additional benefit is small relative to the favorable-subgroup main effect.

analyses.code: Within favorable subgroup, stratify by feature_011
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup (n=9338): feature_011=0 (n=8843) treatment effect = +4.99 mo; feature_011=1 (n=495) treatment effect = +5.24 mo. Difference of ~0.25 mo; small relative to the 5.0 mo main subgroup effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_031 is not associated with pfs_months and does not modify the treatment effect of feature_027.

analyses.code: t-test pfs_months by feature_031; interaction with feature_027
analyses.result_summary: feature_031 main effect: 4.31 (level 0) vs 4.31 (level 1), diff = -0.006, p = 0.77. Treatment-by-feature_031 interaction p = 0.55. Null on both.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous lab features (feature_005, feature_032, feature_017, feature_021, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026) are weakly to moderately intercorrelated, but no single one is a near-deterministic function of another in the cohort.

analyses.code: Spearman correlation matrix on continuous features
analyses.result_summary: All Spearman absolute correlations among continuous features < 0.3; no near-collinearity. The dominant continuous prognostic signal comes from feature_015 (rho = +0.79 with pfs_months); the next largest |rho| with pfs is feature_025 (-0.12) and feature_019 (+0.10). Other continuous features have |rho| < 0.05 with pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients NOT meeting the joint favorable subgroup definition (i.e., any of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_005>5, or feature_022=1), the treatment effect of feature_027 is essentially zero (|effect| < 0.1 months) and the small positive marginal effect is statistical noise driven by huge sample size.

analyses.code: Restrict to unfavorable cohort; t-test pfs by feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Unfavorable cohort (n=40662): treated mean 4.147, untreated mean 4.124, difference +0.024 (p = 0.36). Effect is consistent with noise. The drug should not be expected to help patients meeting any unfavorable predicate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A model with feature_027 alone (no covariate adjustment) plus an interaction term for the joint favorable subgroup explains essentially all of the treatment-related variance: adding the four interaction predicates is highly significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * fav', df).fit() with fav = joint favorable indicator
analyses.result_summary: fav x feature_027 model: feature_027:fav coefficient = +4.99 (p ~ 0). Main effect of feature_027 in the unfavorable group is +0.024 (n.s.). The single interaction term carries virtually all of the treatment-related variance attributed to the four-predicate subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 is the strongest single predictor of pfs_months in the cohort (univariate Spearman rho > 0.5), more so than any other feature including the treatment.

analyses.code: Compare R^2 of pfs ~ feature_015 vs pfs ~ feature_027 etc.
analyses.result_summary: R^2 (univariate) for pfs ~ feature_015 = 0.586 (slope +0.176); for pfs ~ feature_027 ~ 0.018; for pfs ~ feature_001 ~ 0.142; for pfs ~ feature_014 ~ 0.087. feature_015 dominates the prognostic signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 is a strong negative prognostic factor (-1.35 main effect) but does NOT modify the treatment effect of feature_027.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_014)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature_014 interaction p = 0.15. Treatment effect: +0.94 in feature_014=0 vs +1.01 in feature_014=1. feature_014 changes baseline PFS dramatically but does not modify the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 is a strong prognostic factor but does not substantially modify the treatment effect of feature_027.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_001)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature_001 interaction p = 0.17. Subgroup effects: level 0 = +0.93, level 1 = +0.99, level 2 = +0.90. No meaningful interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining the favorable subgroup with only three predicates (feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005<=5) without explicitly including feature_022=0 still recovers an enriched treatment effect of approximately +4.6 months because feature_022=1 within the three-predicate group is rare (~8% of n=10117) and slightly dilutes but does not abolish the enrichment.

analyses.code: Compute treatment effect with 3 predicates vs 4 predicates
analyses.result_summary: Three-predicate subgroup (006=0, 023=0, 005<=5): n=10117, treatment effect +4.624 (p ~ 0). Four-predicate subgroup adding 022=0: n=9338, treatment effect +5.014. Adding 022=0 sharpens the subgroup but isn't required to detect strong enrichment; however the marginal utility of including 022 is real (a non-negligible 8% of three-predicate patients do not benefit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete and best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for this cohort is: feature_027 (treatment) increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months only in patients meeting ALL of feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005<=5.0 AND feature_022=0; in any patient violating any of these four predicates, the treatment confers no clinically or statistically meaningful PFS benefit. Patient prognosis on or off treatment is additionally modulated by intrinsic prognostic factors feature_015 (positive), feature_001 (monotonic negative), feature_014 (negative), feature_019 (positive), feature_025 (negative), and feature_021 (slightly negative), none of which themselves modify the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Composite verification of all preceding analyses.
analyses.result_summary: FINAL: 18.7% of the cohort (n=9338) defines the favorable subgroup. In this group treatment increases pfs_months from 4.09 -> 9.10 (Delta = +5.014, p ~ 0). In the 81.3% complement (n=40662) treatment effect is +0.024 (p=0.36). Within the favorable subgroup, prognostic factors (feature_015, feature_001) continue to operate but do not further modify the treatment effect (interaction p_int > 0.05 for all). Six prognostic factors (feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, feature_021) drive baseline PFS in both arms.
